questions.question: What is the sum of the first 20 natural numbers?
questions.hint: Use the formula n(n+1)/2

questions.question: Find the HCF of 12 and 18.
questions.hint: List the common factors

questions.question: If a shopkeeper sells an article for ₹450 at 10% profit, what was the cost price?
questions.hint: CP = SP / (1 + profit%/100)

questions.question: A number is increased by 20% and then decreased by 20%. The net change is:
questions.hint: Apply successive percentage changes
questions.solution: Let x=100. After 20% increase: 120. After 20% decrease: 120×0.8=96. Net change = -4%

questions.question: A can do a job in 10 days, B in 15 days. How many days will they take working together?
questions.hint: Add their per-day work rates
questions.solution: A's rate=1/10, B's rate=1/15. Combined=1/10+1/15=5/30=1/6. Together=6 days

questions.question: A train 200m long passes a pole in 20 seconds. Its speed in km/h is:
questions.hint: Speed = Distance/Time, convert m/s to km/h

questions.question: A man buys an article for ₹800, sells at 25% profit. He uses that money to buy another article and sells at 20% loss. Overall profit/loss?
questions.hint: Track the money flow step by step

questions.question: If 3x + 7 = 22, what is x?
questions.hint: Isolate x

questions.question: Two dice are thrown. Probability of getting a sum of 7 is:
questions.solution: Favorable outcomes = 6. Total = 36. P = 6/36 = 1/6

questions.question: In how many ways can 5 people sit around a circular table?
questions.hint: Circular permutation = (n-1)!
questions.solution: Circular arrangement = (5-1)! = 4! = 24

questions.question: What comes next in the series: 2, 6, 18, 54, ?
questions.hint: Each number is multiplied by a constant

questions.question: Find the missing number: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
questions.hint: These are perfect squares

questions.question: If COMPUTER is coded as DPNQVUFS, how is MOUSE coded?
questions.hint: Each letter is shifted by +1
questions.solution: Each letter +1: M→N, O→P, U→V, S→T, E→F = NPVTF

questions.question: Pointing to a photo, A says 'He is the son of my father's only daughter.' Who is in the photo?
questions.hint: Father's only daughter = A (if A is female)
questions.solution: A's father's only daughter = A herself. So the person is A's son.

questions.question: A man walks 5km North, turns right and walks 3km, turns right again and walks 5km. Which direction is he facing?
questions.hint: Trace the path on a diagram
questions.solution: North→Right(East)→Right(South). He is facing South.

questions.question: All cats are animals. Some animals are wild. Conclusion: Some cats are wild.
questions.hint: Draw a Venn diagram
questions.solution: The conclusion does not necessarily follow. 'Some animals are wild' doesn't mean the wild ones include cats.

questions.question: Book : Pages :: Tree : ?
questions.hint: A book has many pages, a tree has many...
questions.solution: A book is made of pages. A tree has leaves (many small parts).

questions.question: If 1=5, 2=25, 3=325, 4=4325, then 5=?
questions.hint: Look at the pattern of digits being prepended
questions.solution: Pattern: prepend the number. 5→54325

questions.question: What is the angle between the hour and minute hands at 3:30?
questions.hint: Hour hand moves 0.5° per minute, minute hand moves 6° per minute

questions.question: If January 1, 2023 was a Sunday, what day was March 1, 2023?
questions.hint: Count the days from Jan 1 to Mar 1
questions.solution: Jan has 31 days, Feb has 28 (2023 not leap). 31+28=59 days. 59÷7=8 weeks + 3 days. Sunday+3=Wednesday

questions.question: Choose the synonym of 'Ubiquitous':
questions.hint: Think of something found everywhere
questions.solution: Ubiquitous means 'present everywhere' = Omnipresent

questions.question: Choose the antonym of 'Benevolent':
questions.hint: Benevolent means kind/generous
questions.solution: Benevolent = kind. Opposite = Malevolent (wishing harm)

questions.question: Select the grammatically correct sentence:
questions.hint: Check subject-verb agreement and double negatives
questions.solution: 'He doesn't know anything' - correct subject-verb agreement, no double negative.

questions.question: In the sentence 'The CEO's speech was a catalyst for change,' what does 'catalyst' mean?
questions.hint: A catalyst speeds up reactions
questions.solution: Catalyst = something that triggers or accelerates change = Stimulus

questions.question: Identify the error: 'Each of the students have completed their assignment.'
questions.hint: 'Each' is singular
questions.solution: 'Each' is singular, so verb should be 'has' not 'have'. Correct: 'Each of the students has completed...'

questions.question: The manager ___ the employees for their hard work.
questions.hint: The sentence implies positive recognition
questions.solution: Commended = praised (fits the positive context of recognizing hard work)

questions.question: If an author writes 'The policy proved to be a double-edged sword,' the author implies:
options: The policy had both advantages and disadvantages
questions.hint: Double-edged sword is an idiom
questions.solution: Double-edged sword = something that has both positive and negative consequences

questions.question: Arrange: (A) However, it requires practice. (B) Public speaking is a valuable skill. (C) With dedication, anyone can master it. (D) Many people fear it initially.
questions.hint: Start with the introduction of the topic
questions.solution: B introduces topic → D states the problem → A adds contrast → C concludes. BDAC

questions.question: What does 'to burn the midnight oil' mean?
options: To work or study late at night
questions.hint: Oil lamps were used for lighting before electricity
questions.solution: 'Burn the midnight oil' = to work or study late into the night

questions.question: Choose the word that best fits: 'The professor's ___ lecture left the students confused.'
questions.hint: The result was confusion, so the lecture was...
questions.solution: Abstruse = difficult to understand. Fits the context of students being confused.

questions.question: What is the space complexity of an array of size n?
questions.hint: An array stores n elements
questions.solution: O(n) — An array of size n stores n elements, each occupying constant space.

questions.question: What is the best algorithm to find the majority element (appears > n/2 times)?
questions.hint: This algorithm uses O(1) space and O(n) time
questions.solution: Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm: O(n) time, O(1) space. Maintains a candidate and count.

questions.question: How do you detect a cycle in a linked list optimally?
questions.hint: Uses slow and fast pointers
questions.solution: Floyd's Tortoise and Hare: slow moves 1 step, fast moves 2. If they meet, there's a cycle. O(n) time, O(1) space.

questions.question: Which data structure is best for evaluating postfix expressions?
questions.hint: You process operands and operators in order
questions.solution: Stack — Push operands. When an operator appears, pop two operands, compute, push result.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of finding the Lowest Common Ancestor in a BST?
questions.hint: You traverse from root towards the nodes
questions.solution: O(h) where h is tree height. In balanced BST, h=log n. Navigate left/right based on node values.

questions.question: Which algorithm detects negative weight cycles?
questions.hint: More than one algorithm can do this
questions.solution: Both Bellman-Ford and Floyd-Warshall can detect negative weight cycles.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of the longest common subsequence (LCS) problem?
questions.hint: It fills a 2D DP table
questions.solution: O(m×n) where m and n are the string lengths. The DP table has m×n cells, each computed in O(1).

questions.question: Which sorting algorithm is stable and has O(n log n) worst case?
questions.hint: It divides the array into halves and merges
questions.solution: Merge Sort — Always O(n log n), stable (preserves relative order of equal elements).

questions.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of hash table operations?
questions.hint: What if all keys hash to the same bucket?
questions.solution: O(n) — In worst case, all keys collide to the same bucket, forming a linked list.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of extracting the k-th largest element using a min-heap of size k?
questions.hint: Maintain a heap of size k and process n elements
questions.solution: O(n log k) — Process each of n elements with heap of size k. Each heap operation is O(log k).
